office_does: State representatives write and vote on state laws and budgets, oversee state government, and represent their legislative district.
race_blurb: Incumbent Deb Manjarrez faces Democratic challengers Ezequiel Morfin and Tony G Sandoval.

candidates.summary: Manjarrez is the incumbent representative, a CPA and orchardist who emphasizes agriculture, affordability, public safety, education, and spending accountability.
highlights: Serves as the incumbent representative with documented committee and legislative records [S1][S2]
Emphasizes agriculture, affordability, education, public safety, and accountable spending [S1][S2]

spending.basis: Accountable state spending is an explicit priority, though the dossier gives no detailed audit mechanism.

experience.basis: She is an incumbent with an accessible legislative record and substantial professional experience.

candidates.summary: Morfin is a former Toppenish councilmember and planning commissioner who emphasizes workforce training, housing costs, and immigrant communities.
highlights: Supports apprenticeship and workforce training and lower housing costs [S1]

experience.basis: His city-council and planning-commission service provide a local governing record.

immigration.basis: Advocacy for immigrant communities supplies a protect-immigrants direction without a detailed enforcement policy.

candidates.summary: Sandoval brings management and regulatory-compliance experience and campaigns on practical, bipartisan state administration.
highlights: Reports more than 30 years of community involvement and management experience [S1]
Emphasizes cross-party work and practical management [S1]

experience.basis: He brings professional management experience but no documented elected or legislative record.
